milestoneTitle: Phase M9.19a.7 — Projects pin retired; inventory rolls up to flag pills. The Projects dashboard tile previously used the pin icon to mean 'this project has N tools / M snippets pinned inside it' — a content signal that stole the pin visual and broke the newly-established universal pin semantic ('pin = send to Learn'). Pin is now invisible-reserved on Projects tiles like on Learn tiles; the inventory rollup migrates to the flag row as small caps pills ('2 TOOLS' / '1 SNIPPET') which is the correct home for read-only content signals under the locked layout spec. Flags render using the existing .dash-tile-flag CSS with data-flag values inventory-tools / inventory-snippets (fall through to the default neutral styling; no new CSS needed). Zero tools + zero snippets → no flag row rendered (grid-auto-rows collapses). Universal pin rule now holds cleanly across Learn tiles and Projects dashboard tiles — pin only appears on items OUTSIDE Learn (Tools cards and future Snippets / News / etc.), always meaning 'send to Learn'.
